questions.prompt: Which organ pumps blood through the circulatory system?
What do we call the tiny air sacs in the lungs where gas exchange happens?
questions.hintContext: These are found at the ends of the smallest branches of the lungs.

questions.prompt: Which organ is mainly responsible for filtering waste from the blood and making urine?

questions.hintContext: Think “A” for away from the heart.

questions.prompt: The brain and spinal cord are the main parts of which system?
What is the main job of red blood cells?
Which organ produces bile to help digest fats?
When you breathe in, your rib cage moves outward and your diaphragm moves —
questions.hintContext: This increases space in the chest so the lungs can expand.

questions.prompt: White blood cells are important mainly because they —
choices: help defend the body against germs
questions.answer: help defend the body against germs

questions.prompt: Which state of matter has a definite volume but takes the shape of its container?

questions.hintContext: Often measured in grams or kilograms.

questions.prompt: A mixture of sand and water can be separated by filtering because —
choices: sand is insoluble and the particles are large enough to trap
questions.answer: sand is insoluble and the particles are large enough to trap

questions.prompt: When water boils, it changes from liquid to gas. This is called —
questions.hintContext: Boiling is rapid evaporation at the boiling point.

questions.prompt: Sugar dissolving in tea is best described as forming a —

questions.hintContext: Think about gases blended together in the atmosphere.

questions.prompt: Compressing a gas can increase its — in a fixed volume.
